[agento] fix(scm-github): thread recoveryRef through fetchWithSelfHeal + push tracking after non-AO checkout conflict (#431)

* [agento] fix(scm-github): thread recoveryRef through fetchWithSelfHeal + push tracking after non-AO checkout conflict

Two follow-up fixes for PR #430 ref-based recovery path:

1. fetchWithSelfHeal hardcoded prRef in its non-AO worktree recovery path.
   When called from the branch-name fallback (prRef unavailable), recovery
   retried the same failed prRef. Now accepts optional recoveryRef param so
   the fallback path passes pr.branch as the recovery source instead.

2. After git reset --hard in the checkout conflict path, the session stays on
   its prior branch (session/<id>) while pr.branch is locked in a non-AO
   worktree. Configure git push tracking (branch.<name>.remote + .merge) so
   that git push from the session branch targets pr.branch on the remote.

Fixes: Cursor Bugbot comment #3067902137 and Codex P1 comment #3067898187
Deferred from: https://github.com/jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator/pull/430

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(scm-github): use remote name instead of URL for push tracking and set push.default=upstream

- Change branch.<name>.remote from URL to 'origin' remote name for proper git tracking
- Add push.default=upstream config to handle mismatched local/remote branch names
- Fixes bugs where git push tracking failed due to URL-based remote and simple push.default

* [agento] fix(scm-github): update tests for SHA-based verify + recoveryRef threading + push config

- Replace stale branch-name verify mocks with two rev-parse SHA mocks (deadbeef)
  in tests that call git checkout -f: branch verify is now SHA-based not name-based
- Update non-AO worktree tests (feature-research, ao-9999, /tmp/ao-9999) to reflect
  that recovery path now succeeds instead of throwing (resolves.toBe(true))
- Add test for recoveryRef threading: verifies recovery fetch uses pr.branch (not prRef)
  when fallback fetch is blocked by non-AO worktree
- Add test for push config: verifies branch.<session>.remote uses remote NAME "origin"
  (not URL) and push.default=upstream is set for plain git push to work
- Fix index.ts: use remoteName for branch.<session>.remote config (not URL);
  add git config push.default upstream after reset --hard in non-AO checkout path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [agento] fix(scm-github): add pushDefault mock + assertion for non-AO checkout config test

The rebase from Cursor Agent commit added branch.pushDefault config call alongside
push.default=upstream. Add the matching mock and assertion to the push-tracking test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [copilot] fix(scm-github): use pushRemote per-branch instead of repo-wide push.default

Replace invalid `branch.<session>.pushDefault` (dead git config key) and
repo-wide `push.default=upstream` with `branch.<session>.pushRemote=origin`,
which is scoped to the session branch and does not affect other worktrees.
Add comment explaining that transparent `git push` still requires either
`push.default=upstream` in personal config or explicit
`git push origin HEAD:${pr.branch}`.

Update test: remove stale pushDefault/push.default assertions, add
assertion that `pushRemote` is set and `push.default` is NOT changed globally.

commit.message:
Add regression coverage for narrow cell background width in paginator (#5)

* Add Engine v2 PDF renderer with taffy layout pipeline

Implement full render pipeline: AST → StyledTree (Arena) → LayoutTree
(taffy flexbox/grid) → PageTree (paginator with page breaks) → PDF bytes
(pdf-writer). Fixes cursor double-advancement bug causing overlapping text,
corrects layout width to use content area, adds H2 section spacing and
table header styling. Also adds HTML renderer, QuickLook extension scaffold,
and vertragszusammenfassung example document.

Made-with: Cursor

* Improve PDF rendering: smaller font, tighter spacing, flex-grow columns

Reduce default body font from 12pt to 10pt, line-height from 1.4 to 1.3,
paragraph margin from 4mm to 2.5mm, and page margin from 20mm to 15mm.
Add flex-grow attribute support in resolver and layout so table column
proportions can be controlled per-cell. Update example document with
proper German umlauts and flex-grow: 3 on description columns.

Made-with: Cursor

* feat: literal parens in text, ordered lists, table backgrounds, HR block

- Lexer: literal parentheses in text content are now preserved as-is;
  block detection requires ALL-UPPERCASE idents (H1, TABLE, CELL, etc.),
  so German words like "Informationen (z. B. ...)" no longer parse as blocks.
  Added backslash escape: \( and \) for explicit paren escaping.
- Parser: bare Ident tokens accepted as string values in attrs
  ({type: ordered}, {align: center} work without quotes).
- LIST({type: ordered}): numbered lists rendered as "1. / 2. / 3."
- TABLE/ROW/CELL background-color: ROW({background-color: #0070c0}) and
  CELL({background-color: #ddd}) both render colored backgrounds.
  ROW background overrides default header highlight.
- HR(): horizontal rule block draws a full-width grey line with 3mm margins.
- Example updated: blue header row, green discount cells, ordered speed list,
  HR separators, and restored parentheses in German text.

Made-with: Cursor

* feat: integrate ttf-parser for accurate glyph advance widths

Add ttf-parser dependency and replace the fixed 0.55 character width
approximation in break_text with real per-glyph advance metrics loaded
from system fonts (Helvetica Neue / Helvetica / Arial fallback).
Metrics are lazily cached via OnceLock for regular and bold faces.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(html): light document theme for QuickLook, add LIST/ITEM rendering

Replace hardcoded dark theme (#0f0f12) with a clean white paper style
that matches an actual document preview. WKWebView now shows white
background regardless of system dark mode (color-scheme: light).
Also: render LIST/ITEM as ul/ol+li, fix background-color attr in
build_inline_style, fix grid-template-columns to emit correct fr units.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: remove stray ET op and add WinAnsi cp1252 char mappings

Drop the unpaired Op::EndTextSection after the block loop in the legacy
renderer — write_text already wraps every call in BT/ET, making the extra
ET invalid per PDF spec. Also extend encode_latin1 with explicit cp1252
mappings for common Unicode punctuation (bullets, dashes, smart quotes)
so they round-trip correctly through built-in Type1 fonts instead of
being replaced with '?'.

Made-with: Cursor

* Remove dead FontManager; fix unitless CSS length values in HTML renderer

- Drop FontManager struct and its unused find_font/load methods from
  engine/text.rs — font resolution is handled entirely by the
  load_metrics/get_metrics/GlyphMetrics system
- value_to_css_inline: append "pt" to bare Value::Number so font-size,
  margin and padding produce valid CSS in standards mode
- Add value_to_css_unitless for font-weight where a bare number is correct

Made-with: Cursor

* Fix missing WinAnsiEncoding on built-in Type1 fonts in PDF backend

Without an explicit Encoding entry, PDF viewers fall back to
StandardEncoding where bytes 0x91–0x97 are undefined. encode_latin1
maps smart quotes, bullet, en-dash and em-dash into those cp1252
positions, so they rendered as blank glyphs. Adding
/Encoding /WinAnsiEncoding to both font dictionaries aligns the viewer's
byte interpretation with encode_latin1's output.

Made-with: Cursor

* Refactor parser AST API and complete engine v2 backend transition.

This switches parser internals to an arena-based document API, integrates rustybuzz text shaping, removes legacy printpdf paths, and adds painter/WGPU backend scaffolding while keeping the PDF pipeline unified through engine v2.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add render and text memoization caches.

This introduces document-level PDF render caching and bounded text shaping/line-break caches to reduce repeated layout work on unchanged or repeated content.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add unified export facade and SVG backend.

This introduces format-aware engine export options with shared caching, adds an SVG backend on the same page pipeline, and optimizes font source loading to reduce repeated system font overhead.

Made-with: Cursor

* Implement inline layout v1 and groundwork for advanced engine milestones.

This introduces inline AST/runs, mixed-style line breaking and rendering for PDF/SVG, adds typography and pagination controls, and establishes counters/introspection plus parity quality gates to support the next layout phases.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add CLI printer listing and native print command.

This adds printers discovery plus print execution with lp/lpr fallback and dry-run support for generated PDFs.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add large showcase document and rendered PDF sample.

This includes a feature-rich .fol example and its exported PDF counterpart for size and output comparison.

Made-with: Cursor

* Fix background width and remove redundant render pass

* Fix table child rendering and page root breaks

* Fix WinAnsi mapping, justification width, and text width cache key

* test: add regression for narrow cell background width

Co-authored-by: Artem <fallowlone@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
CI: update GitHub Actions and add zizmor workflow scan (#13)

* ci: bump GitHub Actions and add zizmor workflow scan

- Upgrade checkout and setup-java to current major versions (Node 24 runtime)
- Switch JDK distribution from adopt to temurin for setup-java v5
- Set explicit minimal permissions on the Maven workflow job
- Add zizmor static analysis for workflow configuration issues (annotations, no Advanced Security upload)

Co-authored-by: Mike Houston <mike@kothar.net>

* ci: pin setup-java v5.2.0 and grant actions:write for Maven cache

- Pinned action avoids ambiguous v5 resolution; v5 uses cache client compatible with GitHub cache API (fixes 400 from v2).
- actions: write is required to save/restore caches when workflow permissions are restricted.

feat: Walker mounting + PilotingState (MVP Step 5) (#7)

* feat: implement Walker mounting + PilotingState (MVP Step 5)

- Add activeWalker: WalkerMech | null to GameContext
- Add movement API to WalkerMech: mount/dismount, heading-based steering,
  tier-tuned speed/accel/turn-rate, terrain clamping, walk-phase animation
- Extend CameraRig with dual FP/TP modes: TP chase camera with spring-
  smoothed follow, configurable distance/height per Walker tier
- Create PilotingState: WASD drives Walker, TP camera orbits hull,
  compass to nearest POI, hold E to dismount back to ExploringState
- Wire ExploringState activation flow: activate -> set activeWalker ->
  requestStateChange('piloting'). Re-mount activated Walkers with hold E.
- Register PilotingState in Game.ts alongside ExploringState
- Update architecture.md with all new/changed files

Co-authored-by: Hunter Bastian <hunterbastianux@gmail.com>

* feat: add GLASSWAKE title screen with cinematic camera drift

- TitleScreen overlay: GLASSWAKE in Barlow Condensed, tagline, pulsing
  'Click to begin' prompt. Fades out on click/Space/Enter.
- Cinematic camera: slow orbit above spawn point at 45m height, gentle
  vertical bob, looking slightly ahead of orbit center.
- Live world behind title: sky, wind, water, vegetation, grass, clouds,
  campfires, Walker idle animations all running during title.
- Audio starts on title dismiss (click satisfies browser autoplay policy).
- HUD hidden during title, fades in on dismiss.
- Pointer lock requested on dismiss to start gameplay immediately.
- CSS: vignette overlay, fade-in animation, responsive font sizing,
  consistent with existing Frutiger Aero / Mirror's Edge design system.
- Updated architecture.md and Game.ts entry.

Co-authored-by: Hunter Bastian <hunterbastianux@gmail.com>

* feat: golden hour start, spawn facing Walker, cinematic activation

1. Lock golden hour on start:
   - SkySystem.timeOfDay starts at 0.72 (late afternoon golden light)
   - freezeTime stays true during title screen
   - Day/night cycle unfreezes on title dismiss

2. Spawn facing nearest Walker:
   - Camera yaw computed from spawn → nearest Walker direction
   - First thing player sees is the dormant Walker in the distance

3. Cinematic Walker activation (6 seconds):
   - Camera locks to dramatic orbit angle around Walker
   - Bloom spikes from 2.5× to 3.5× then fades
   - Screen shake builds during rise, peaks, settles
   - Walker rises from sunken pose (cubic ease-out)
   - 40 dust particles burst outward with gravity + fade
   - Camera pulls back to wide shot
   - PostFX.setBloomOverride() for cinematic bloom control
   - ActivationCinematic class manages full sequence
   - On complete: transitions to PilotingState

Supporting changes:
   - GameContext now includes postfx + audio refs
   - PostFX gains setBloomOverride(intensity | null)
   - Updated architecture.md

Co-authored-by: Hunter Bastian <hunterbastianux@gmail.com>

* fix: terrain/ground visibility (depth + SSAO + fog biome pass)

- Enable logarithmicDepthBuffer on WebGLRenderer for large-world depth precision
- Soften SSAO (lower intensity, higher bias/threshold) — was crushing flat ground
- GrassField: depthWrite false so blades don't corrupt depth for terrain behind
- OutlineShell: depthWrite false + polygon offset — Walker outlines no longer steal depth
- Terrain mesh: PostFX.tagBiome(plains) so height-fog biome pass isn't wrong-default

commit.message:
feat(commands): add 6 lifecycle slash commands (#1611)

* feat(commands): add 6 lifecycle slash commands, remove workflow/

Add Osmani-pattern lifecycle commands for Claude Code:
- /spec: problem to requirements with CVA analysis
- /plan: specs to milestones with risk coverage
- /build: TDD implementation with atomic commits
- /test: layered testing with hypothesis debugging
- /review: 5-axis review (architecture, security, quality, tests, standards)
- /ship: pre-flight validation delegating to /push-pr

Remove unused workflow/ commands (0-init through 9-sync). Update AGENTS.md
and CLAUDE.md routing. Exclude .claude/commands/ from markdownlint MD041.

Closes #1609

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct skill routing and lifecycle command consistency

- Fix CLAUDE.md skill routing to reference actual skills (analyze, github,
  pr-comment-responder, reflect, session-end, quality-grades) and separate
  lifecycle commands (/spec, /plan, /build, /test, /review, /ship) into
  their own section
- Add @CLAUDE.md import to spec.md, plan.md, test.md, and ship.md for
  consistent project context loading
- Add Write and Edit to test.md allowed-tools to enable writing tests
  and fixing code as documented in steps 5-6

* fix: narrow markdownlint ignore and correct skill references in ship command

- .markdownlint-cli2.yaml: Replace overly broad .claude/commands/** ignore
  with specific list of 6 new lifecycle command files. This preserves
  full linting for pre-existing command files (push-pr.md, pr-review.md,
  validate-pr-description.md, etc.)

- .claude/commands/ship.md: Correct references to validate-pr-description
  and push-pr. These are slash commands (.claude/commands/*.md), not
  skills (.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md). Update instructions to use command
  invocation syntax instead of Skill tool references.

* refactor(commands): apply prompt engineering patterns to all 6 lifecycle commands

- Add role personas to every Task(subagent_type) invocation
- Move skill invocations from top-level directives into process steps
- Add empty $ARGUMENTS guards to all commands
- Add @CLAUDE.md import to all 6 for consistency
- Add PR type classification to /test (skip irrelevant gates)
- Add structured verdict output format to /test and /ship
- Add Write to spec.md and plan.md allowed-tools
- Add Bash(uv:*) to build.md and test.md, Bash(gh:*) to ship.md
- Fix review.md hard-coded main branch reference
- Expand test.md from 5 axes to 6 quality gates with multi-agent dispatch
- Update prompt-engineer skill to claude-sonnet-4-6

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use dynamic base branch detection in /test and add gh permission to /review

- test.md: Add Bash(gh:*) to allowed-tools and update Step 0 to detect
  base branch from gh pr view --json baseRefName with fallback to main
- review.md: Add Bash(gh:*) to allowed-tools to enable base branch
  detection that was already documented but not permitted

* fix(commands): use /command syntax instead of Skill() for command references

ship.md referenced validate-pr-description and push-pr via Skill() invocation
but both are slash commands, not skills. Changed to /command syntax for clarity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore architecture review routing and correct Pester tool pattern

- Add 'architecture review' trigger to /review routing in CLAUDE.md
- Replace Bash(pester:*) with Bash(pwsh:*) in test.md since Pester is
  invoked via pwsh, not a standalone pester binary

* fix(commands): use Bash(*) for platform/stack portability, deprecate workflow skill

- All action commands (build, test, review, ship) now use Bash(*) to support
  any tech stack (npm, cargo, go, dotnet, uv, etc.) and any git host
  (GitHub, Azure DevOps, BitBucket) on any OS (Linux, macOS, Windows).
- Read-only commands (spec, plan) keep no Bash at all.
- Deprecate workflow skill SKILL.md (commands deleted, scripts retained).
- Address stale workflow reference from Copilot review comment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix+feat: Complete systematic overhaul — all items implemented including LLM report generation (#199)

* fix: use STORAGES dict for WhiteNoise in Django 6 (STATICFILES_STORAGE ignored)

Django 4.2 deprecated STATICFILES_STORAGE in favour of the STORAGES dict.
Django 6 silently ignores STATICFILES_STORAGE entirely, meaning
CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage was never activated in production —
static files were served uncompressed and unhashed.

Replace STATICFILES_STORAGE with STORAGES in both settings.py and
settings_universal.py so WhiteNoise compression and cache-busting hashing
are correctly applied when DEBUG=False.

Co-authored-by: noctispro6-cloud <noctispro6-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: migrate pydicom.pixel_data_handlers.util to pydicom.pixels

pydicom.pixel_data_handlers will be removed in pydicom v4.0.
Replace all imports of apply_voi_lut and convert_color_space from
the deprecated pydicom.pixel_data_handlers.util with the new stable
location pydicom.pixels.

- dicom_viewer/views.py (3 import sites: top-level + 2 inline)

* fix: remove broken gdcm==1.1 from requirements.txt

The PyPI gdcm package (version 1.1) fails to import with:
  ModuleNotFoundError: No module named '_gdcmswig'
because it requires libgdcmMEXD.so.2.8 which is not present in
standard Linux environments.

gdcm is not imported anywhere in the application source code.
DICOM pixel decoding is handled by pydicom + pylibjpeg + pylibjpeg-libjpeg
+ pylibjpeg-openjpeg which are all working correctly.

Co-authored-by: noctispro6-cloud <noctispro6-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove dead SECURE_BROWSER_XSS_FILTER setting; update docs URL to Django 6.0

SECURE_BROWSER_XSS_FILTER was removed from Django's SecurityMiddleware
in Django 4.0. The setting has had no effect since then. Keeping it
in the codebase was misleading — developers could believe the X-XSS-Protection
header was being sent when it was not.

Also update the stale Django 5.2 docs URL in the settings.py docstring
to point to the correct Django 6.0 documentation.

commit.message:
fix(rpc): fix seer RPC exception handling to default to 500 and preserve rest APIException (#112435) ## Problem When Seer makes RPC calls to Sentry and encounters downstream errors (like 429 rate limits or 500 errors from Snuba), the current code catches all exceptions and re-raises them as `ValidationError` (400). This misrepresents the actual issue, making it appear as if the client sent bad request parameters when the real cause is a downstream service error. Example issue: https://sentry.dev.getsentry.net:7999/issues/7380803020/events/d3e6e1d5ddf44d7b94c86e6ce2435ea6/ ## Solution This PR fixes the exception handling in the seer RPC endpoint to raise a generic 500 APIException by default, implying an error happened on sentry server. Also re-raises APIException's (base class for rest framework excs) so developers can bubble their own status codes up to response for Seer. 1. **Added a handler to re-raise REST framework exceptions directly** - Any `APIException` (which includes `Throttled`, custom API exceptions, etc.) is now re-raised as-is, preserving its status code 2. **Changed generic exception handler to raise `APIException` (500) instead of `ValidationError` (400)** - Unknown errors now correctly return 500 Internal Server Error instead of 400 Bad Request ### Changes - Modified `SeerRpcServiceEndpoint.post()` in `src/sentry/seer/endpoints/seer_rpc.py`: - Added `except APIException: raise` handler before the generic exception handler - Changed `raise ValidationError from e` to `raise APIException from e` for generic exceptions - Added test cases in `tests/sentry/seer/endpoints/test_seer_rpc.py`: - `test_rest_framework_exceptions_are_reraised` - verifies custom API exceptions preserve their status codes - `test_generic_exceptions_return_500` - verifies generic exceptions return 500 instead of 400 ## Testing Pre-commit checks pass ✅ [Slack Thread](https://sentry.slack.com/archives/C0AAF0JD0GK/p1775600719171269?thread_ts=1775600719.171269&cid=C0AAF0JD0GK)
  
--------- Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent Co-authored-by: Andrew Liu

fix: address multiple GitHub issues (#117, #113, #119, #106) (#120)

* fix: detect namespace hook calls (React.useState, React.useEffect) in all rules

Fixes #117: isHookCall now handles MemberExpression callee types
(e.g. React.useEffect, React.useState) in addition to plain Identifier
callee types. This fixes silent misses in no-derived-state-effect,
no-fetch-in-effect, no-cascading-set-state, no-effect-event-handler,
rerender-functional-setstate, rendering-hydration-no-flicker, and
the countSetStateCalls helper.

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove unsupported jsx-a11y/no-noninteractive-element-interactions rule

Fixes #113, #119: This rule does not exist in the bundled oxlint
binary, causing 'Failed to parse oxlint configuration file' errors
and score suppression on all scans.

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add --no flag to skip prompts and always run full scan

Fixes #106: Adds -n/--no flag that skips all interactive prompts
(like --yes) but specifically declines the diff-only scan prompt,
ensuring a full project scan runs. Useful for CI agents that cannot
interact with TTY prompts.

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add namespace hook detection tests (React.useEffect, React.useState, etc.)

Adds 13 tests verifying that all rules correctly detect namespace-style
hook calls (import * as React; React.useEffect, React.useState, etc.).
Covers: no-derived-state-effect, no-fetch-in-effect, no-cascading-set-state,
no-effect-event-handler, no-derived-useState, rerender-lazy-state-init,
rerender-functional-setstate, rerender-dependencies, rendering-hydration-no-flicker,
no-usememo-simple-expression, prefer-useReducer, plus a regression guard for
direct-import fixtures.

Relates to #117.

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add edge case tests for project discovery and catalog resolution

Adds tests for:
- peerDependencies-only with catalog reference resolution
- Catalog reference to nonexistent catalog name (fallback to default)
- Empty pnpm-workspace.yaml handling
- Malformed package.json in workspace subdirectories
- Nonexistent root directory for discoverReactSubprojects
- File entries mixed with directory entries in project root

Relates to #101, #87, #105, #71.

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: handle malformed JSON in workspace package.json files gracefully

readPackageJson now catches SyntaxError from malformed JSON and returns
an empty object instead of crashing. This prevents workspace discovery
from aborting when a subdirectory has an invalid package.json.

Previously, a broken package.json in any workspace subdirectory would
crash the entire scan with an unhandled SyntaxError.

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add customRulesOnly config to disable builtin react/jsx-a11y rules

Fixes #109: Teams with existing ESLint setups can now set
customRulesOnly: true in react-doctor.config.json to only run
react-doctor/* custom rules, skipping the bundled react/, jsx-a11y/,
react-perf, and react-compiler rules.

This avoids rule duplication for projects that already lint with
eslint-plugin-react, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y, etc.

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: inherit config from ancestor directories for monorepo support

Fixes #73: loadConfig now walks up the directory tree to find
react-doctor.config.json or package.json reactDoctor key from
ancestor directories. This means a single config at the monorepo
root applies to all workspace packages without needing to duplicate
the config in each package.

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add --annotations flag for GitHub Actions annotation output

Fixes #81: The --annotations flag outputs diagnostics in GitHub
Actions annotation format (::error / ::warning) so they appear
as inline annotations on PR diffs. Example:
  ::error file=src/App.tsx,line=42,title=react-doctor/no-fetch-in-effect::fetch() inside useEffect

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove Ami integration

Removes all Ami-related code:
- --ami flag, --fix flag, fix subcommand, install-ami subcommand
- Ami detection, installation, deeplinks, and open-URL helpers
- Fix prompt (maybePromptFix) with Ami banners

- AMI_WEBSITE_URL, AMI_INSTALL_URL, AMI_RELEASES_URL, OPEN_BASE_URL,

CLI bundle reduced by ~39KB (324KB → 285KB).

* fix: recognize custom text components in rn-no-raw-text rule

Fixes #93: The rn-no-raw-text rule now recognizes a much wider set of
text-handling components:

1. Expanded built-in names: Typography, Paragraph, Span, H1-H6
2. Keyword matching instead of suffix-only: any component whose name
   contains Text, Title, Label, Heading, Caption, Subtitle, Typography,
   Paragraph, Description, or Body is treated as text-handling
   (e.g. StyledText, BodyText, CustomLabel, MyTypography)
3. New textComponents config option: users can specify arbitrary
   component names in react-doctor.config.json that should be treated
   as text-handling:

   {
     "textComponents": ["MyCustomComp", "NativeWindText"]
   }

   Diagnostics inside these components are filtered out post-lint.

* fix: add file count limit per oxlint batch to prevent OOM crashes

Mitigates #84/#77: Adds OXLINT_MAX_FILES_PER_BATCH (500) to cap the
number of files per oxlint invocation. Combined with the oxlint upgrade
from 1.47 to 1.59, this prevents SIGABRT/OOM crashes on large projects
with 100+ source files.

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add --staged flag for pre-commit hook scanning

Fixes #115: The --staged flag scans only files in the git index
(staged with git add). It materializes staged content to a temp
directory so oxlint reads the exact index version, not the working
tree version. This is critical for pre-commit hooks where the working
tree may differ from what's being committed.

Usage:
  npx react-doctor . --staged --fail-on error

For lint-staged:
  "*.{tsx,jsx}": "npx react-doctor . --staged --fail-on error"

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: code review fixes per AGENTS.md rules

- DRY: Extract shared createFileLinesCache in filter-diagnostics.ts
- DRY: Extract resolveFailOnLevel in cli.ts (was duplicated in
  staged and normal paths)
- DRY: Reuse exported getCalleeName from helpers.ts instead of
  duplicating in state-and-effects.ts and performance.ts
- Magic number: Move maxBuffer 10MB to GIT_SHOW_MAX_BUFFER_BYTES constant
- Variable naming: prevLine → previousLine, tagName → fullTagName,
  leafName → leafTagName, configFiles → projectConfigFilenames
- Remove .pop()! non-null assertion in favor of .at(-1) with fallback

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent shell injection in staged file content reading

Replace execSync with string interpolation with spawnSync using an
args array. File paths with shell metacharacters (quotes, semicolons,
etc.) in their names are now passed safely without shell interpretation.

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: pass resolved config to staged scan to preserve ancestor inheritance

The staged scan materializes files to /tmp, where loadConfig's ancestor
traversal would never find a monorepo root's config. Now the CLI resolves
config from the real project directory first and passes it via the new
configOverride ScanOption, so customRulesOnly, share, ignore rules,
and textComponents all apply correctly during --staged scans.

Co-authored-by: Aiden Bai <aidenybai@users.noreply.github.com>
